highlight.title: Next.js 16.1 Turbopack Stable: AI-Ready Development
highlight.summary: Next.js 16.1 introduces stable Turbopack with file system caching, AI-ready create-next-app templates, and experimental Agent DevTools. The release delivers 10x faster builds while preparing developers for AI-integrated workflows and agent-powered debugging.

resources.title: Next.js 15/16 Migration Guide: Server Components & Partial Prerendering
resources.why_now: Next.js 15+ is now production-ready with React 19 support and Turbopack stability. The migration patterns for Server Components and async request APIs represent breaking changes that senior developers need to understand for 2026 projects.

resources.title: TanStack Start RC: Client-First Alternative to Next.js
resources.why_now: TanStack Start challenges Next.js's server-first paradigm with a client-first approach and type-safe RPC. It's gaining traction among teams building interactive applications who find React Server Components too opinionated.

resources.title: TypeScript 6.0: Final JavaScript-Based Release Before Go Native 7.0
resources.why_now: TypeScript 6.0 is the bridge to TypeScript 7.0's Go-based compiler promising 10x build performance. Senior developers need to prepare for deprecations and the ecosystem shift to native compilation that fundamentally changes the development workflow.

resources.title: Full-Stack TypeScript Patterns: T3 Stack Dominance in 2026
resources.why_now: The T3 stack (Next.js + TypeScript + tRPC + Prisma) has become the default for type-safe full-stack development. The pattern of schema-to-UI type inference eliminates manual type annotations and represents how modern teams achieve end-to-end type safety.

claude_take: We're witnessing a pivotal moment where TypeScript transitions from JavaScript add-on to platform foundation with native execution and dramatically faster compilation. Next.js continues its server-first evolution while alternatives like TanStack Start provide escape routes for teams preferring client-centric architectures.
